description:
To properly set the permissions of /usr/bin/lastlog, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0750 /usr/bin/lastlog
rationale:
Unauthorized disclosure of the contents of the /var/log/lastlog file can reveal system data to
attackers, thus compromising its confidentiality.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /usr/bin/lastlog, run the command: 
$ ls -l /usr/bin/lastlog
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: -rwxr-x---
fixtext:
Change the permissions of the directory "/usr/bin/lastlog/" to "0750" by running the following command:
$ sudo chmod 0750 /usr/bin/lastlog/
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /usr/bin/lastlog directory must have mode 0750 or less permissive.
title: Verify Permissions on lastlog Command
